response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is a technically accomplished individual contributor with 17+ years of development experience and genuine depth in developer tooling, test infrastructure, and TDD practices — all relevant to the role's focus area. However, this is explicitly a people management position requiring at least 2 years of managing direct reports, and the resume contains no evidence of this whatsoever: no mention of hiring, performance reviews, one-on-ones, or leading a team. Staff-level titles and a founder role suggest informal technical seniority but do not substitute for the people management track record a Level II Manager position demands. The gap is too fundamental to screen past.
strengths: Genuine developer tooling and test infrastructure background: built Fluent-Asserts, Trial test runner, CI/CD pipelines at Altom, and MCP code generators at re:cinq — directly on-target for engineering productivity

Consistent record of knowledge-sharing through workshops, conference talks, and a 3-module Selenium course, indicating the coaching and communication instincts that underpin effective engineering management
Broad multi-platform exposure including iOS/Apple TV development, mobile device orchestration, and embedded systems, which partially satisfies the preferred mobile-platform knowledge
concerns: Zero evidence of people management: no direct reports, no hiring, no performance feedback cycles, no team budget or headcount responsibility — the single most critical gap for a Level II Manager role
No demonstrated experience owning a team-level technical roadmap, setting organizational priorities, or driving cross-team alignment at any scale
No advanced degree and no evidence of the organizational-change leadership (3 years preferred) or system-design validation responsibilities expected at senior management level

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling and test infrastructure depth
No roadmap ownership or org leadership
